title: Shaded closeout input for WS1 handoff
body: Team member closeout from Shaded Shrouding Mask for the owner-requested WS1 handoff. Boundary owned: WS1 implementation synthesis in agent-tools practice-fitness source plus validation evidence before closing claim ed9e4df9. Outcome: WS1 is implemented and validation-green in the working tree; reusable Practice fitness logic now lives under agent-tools/src/practice-fitness; validate-practice-fitness is a thin compatibility wrapper; reports render content-only chars/4 Tokens rows; no WS2 frontmatter fields, convention docs, or manifest coverage were started. Evidence: focused src/practice-fitness Vitest, @oaknational/agent-tools type-check, lint, build, practice:fitness:informational, and markdownlint on the controlling plan all passed during my WS1 window. Claims / queue / git state at my closeout: my WS1 claim was closed; active queue was empty; the tree remained dirty with WS1 source plus shared collaboration-state changes. Surprise or changed understanding: multiple Codex sessions independently opened overlapping WS1 claims and implementations, so the effective blocker was not code complexity but ownership collision and shared-state churn. Blockers or risks: exact pathspec commit must happen before WS2, and the commit owner must preserve peer collaboration-state cleanup rather than absorbing or reverting it accidentally. Positives: independent agents converged on the same content-only/raw-file distinction and the modular implementation now has focused tests. Handoff needed: Floating Lifting Thermal currently owns the canonical handoff claim; please include this as supporting synthesis and keep no git/index work in this handoff unless the owner redirects.
